---
title: "data modeling by Yaxing Li"
author: "Yaxing Li"
date: "2025-03-07"
output: html_document
---

# Modeling

## 1.Preparation of data and packages
```{R}
library(rBayesianOptimization)
library(ParBayesianOptimization)
library(torch)    # PyTorch for R
library(torchvision)  # Data transformations (if needed)
library(keras)    # Keras for deep learning
library(tensorflow) # TensorFlow backend
library(tidymodels)  # Preprocessing and workflows
library("tidyverse")
library("tidymodels")
sessionInfo()
data <- read_csv("final_engineered_data.csv")
```

## 2.transforming the label variable
```{R}
# Transforming the label variable
data |> dim()
data |> head()

# Split the data into training and test set
train_data <- data %>% 
  filter(split == "train") %>% 
  mutate(label=factor(label, levels=c('baseline','meditation','stress','break')))  # Convert the label column to factor
train_data <- train_data %>% select(-split)
test_data <- data %>% 
  filter(split == "test")
test_data <- test_data %>% select(-split)
k_folds <- 5  # You can adjust this value for the number of folds you want
set.seed(123)  # For reproducibility
cv_splits <- vfold_cv(train_data, v = k_folds, strata = label)

```


## 3. Create the workflow
```{R}
# Define the model building function
build_model <- function(lr = 0.001, dropout_rate = 0.3, units_1 = 128, units_2 = 64, input_dim) {
  input_layer <- layer_input(shape = input_dim)
  
  model <- input_layer %>%
    layer_dense(units = units_1, activation = "relu") %>%
    layer_dropout(rate = dropout_rate) %>%
    layer_dense(units = units_2, activation = "relu") %>%
    layer_dropout(rate = dropout_rate) %>%
    layer_dense(units = 4, activation = "softmax")  # 4 categories in the output layer
  
  model <- keras_model(inputs = input_layer, outputs = model)
  
  model %>% compile(
    loss = "sparse_categorical_crossentropy",
    optimizer = optimizer_adam(learning_rate = lr),
    metrics = c("accuracy")
  )
  
  return(model)
}

objective_function <- function(lr, dropout_rate, units_1, units_2) {
  
  # Prepare the recipe for preprocessing
  train_recipe <- recipe(label ~ ., data = train_data) %>%
    update_role(chunk_id, new_role = "ID") %>%
    update_role(participant_id, new_role = "ID") %>%
    step_naomit(all_predictors()) %>%
    step_mutate_at(all_numeric_predictors(), fn = ~replace(., is.infinite(.), NA)) %>%
    step_impute_knn(all_numeric_predictors(), neighbors = 20) %>%
    step_normalize(all_numeric_predictors()) %>%
    step_dummy(all_nominal_predictors()) %>%
    step_corr(all_numeric_predictors(), threshold = 0.8) %>%
    step_zv(all_predictors()) 
  
  # Prepare the recipe and apply transformations to training data
  prepped_recipe <- prep(train_recipe, training = train_data)
  train_processed <- bake(prepped_recipe, new_data = train_data)
  
  # Convert labels to numeric (for Keras model)
  train_processed$label <- as.numeric(as.factor(train_processed$label)) - 1

  # Separate predictors and targets
  X_train <- train_processed %>% select(-label)
  y_train <- train_processed$label

  # Convert to matrix format
  X_train_matrix <- as.matrix(X_train)

  # Build and compile the model with current hyperparameters
  model <- build_model(lr, dropout_rate, units_1, units_2, ncol(X_train_matrix))

  # Fit the model
  history <- model %>% fit(
    X_train_matrix, y_train,
    epochs = 10,
    batch_size = 32,
    validation_split = 0.2,
    verbose = 0,
    callbacks = list(callback_early_stopping(monitor = "val_loss", patience = 5))
  )

  # Return the validation accuracy in a list format
  val_accuracy <- max(history$metrics$val_accuracy)
  return(list(Score = val_accuracy))  # Ensure returning in a list format
}

# Define the search space for Bayesian Optimization
bounds <- list(
  lr = c(1e-5, 1e-2),               # Learning rate between 1e-5 and 1e-2
  dropout_rate = c(0, 0.5),         # Dropout rate between 0 and 0.5
  units_1 = c(32, 256),             # Number of units in the first dense layer
  units_2 = c(32, 256)              # Number of units in the second dense layer
)

# Perform Bayesian optimization
set.seed(123)  # For reproducibility
bayes_opt <- BayesianOptimization(
  FUN = objective_function,
  bounds = bounds,
  init_points = 5,  # Number of random initial points to sample
  n_iter = 25,      # Number of optimization iterations
  acq = "ei",       # Acquisition function (Expected Improvement)
  kappa = 2.5,      # Exploration parameter
  eps = 0.0         # Tolerance level
)

# After optimization, you can access the best hyperparameters and fit the model
best_params <- bayes_opt$Best_Par
```
## 8.Train the final model with the best parameters
```{R}
# Prepare the recipe for preprocessing
train_recipe <- recipe(label ~ ., data = train_data) %>%
  update_role(chunk_id, new_role = "ID") %>%
  update_role(participant_id, new_role = "ID") %>%
  step_naomit(all_predictors()) %>%
  step_mutate_at(all_numeric_predictors(), fn = ~replace(., is.infinite(.), NA)) %>%
  step_impute_knn(all_numeric_predictors(), neighbors = 20) %>%
  step_normalize(all_numeric_predictors()) %>%
  step_dummy(all_nominal_predictors()) %>%
  step_corr(all_numeric_predictors(), threshold = 0.8) %>%
  step_zv(all_predictors())|> 
  step_select(-participant_id, -chunk_id)  # Exclude these columns from preprocessing

# Prepare the recipe and apply transformations to training data
prepped_recipe <- prep(train_recipe, training = train_data)
train_processed <- bake(prepped_recipe, new_data = train_data)

# Convert labels to numeric (for Keras model)
train_processed$label <- as.numeric(as.factor(train_processed$label)) - 1

# Separate predictors and targets
X_train <- train_processed %>% select(-label)
y_train <- train_processed$label

# Convert to matrix format
X_train_matrix <- as.matrix(X_train)

# Now, define the input_dim based on the number of features in X_train_matrix
input_dim <- ncol(X_train_matrix)

# Build the final model with the best parameters and the correct input dimension
final_model <- build_model(
  lr = best_params[["lr"]], 
  dropout_rate = best_params[["dropout_rate"]], 
  units_1 = best_params[["units_1"]], 
  units_2 = best_params[["units_2"]],
  input_dim = input_dim
)


```

## Make Predictions and Evalute the Model
```{R}
# Fit the model
final_model %>% fit(
  X_train_matrix, y_train,
  epochs = 10,
  batch_size = 32,
  verbose = 1
)

# Apply the same preprocessing steps to the test data

X_test_processed <- bake(prepped_recipe, new_data = test_data)
X_test_no_label <- X_test_processed %>% select(-label)
# Convert to matrix format
X_test_matrix <- as.matrix(X_test_no_label)
# Now make predictions with the final model
predictions <- final_model %>% predict(X_test_matrix)

# Convert predictions to class labels
pred_class <- apply(predictions, 1, which.max) - 1  # Subtract 1 for the correct class index
pred_labels <- factor(pred_class, levels = c(0, 1, 2, 3), labels = c('baseline', 'meditation', 'stress', 'break'))

# Create a data frame with participant_id, chunk_id, and predicted labels
submission_data <- data.frame(
  participant_id = test_data$participant_id,
  chunk_id = test_data$chunk_id,
  label = pred_labels  # Using the factor labels
)

table(submission_data$label)

# Save predictions
submission_data %>%
  write.csv('DSW25_CNN_model_with_kfold.csv', row.names = FALSE)
```
